text: This paper presents the design and implementation of Stanford CoreNLP, an integrated suite of natural language processing tools organized as an extensible pipeline. By providing a broad range of core analysis components—spanning from basic tokenization and POS tagging to full syntactic parsing and coreference resolution—the toolkit has become a foundational resource for the NLP research community, as well as for commercial and government sectors. We attribute the widespread adoption of Stanford CoreNLP to its focused, approachable design: it offers straightforward interfaces and robust, high-quality linguistic models without requiring the significant overhead or architectural "baggage" often associated with larger framework ecosystems. In this work, we detail the system's underlying architecture, its ease of use across various programming environments, and the specific annotators that enable sophisticated text analysis.
